steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: Left side: In a 2022 study, researchers is a subject noun phrase. Right side: for a relationship between... is a prepositional phrase, followed by analyzed geotagged social media posts from Los Angeles, a finite verb with its object.

steps.title: Apply the participial phrase rule
steps.content: A participial phrase modifies the subject noun, with the sentence's main finite verb being separate. Option B uses looking, a present participle modifying researchers, allowing analyzed to be the main verb of the sentence: Researchers looking for a relationship...analyzed geotagged social media posts. Options A (had looked), C (were looking), and D (looked) use finite verbs that compete with analyzed as main verbs. That matches choice B — B is correct.
